methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Avner and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money on $13.5M raised. The valuation is anchored against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 2.5x multiplier from Seed to next stage applied to the $60M last post-money, implies a forward value of $150M. Peer-median analysis compares Tenna against three same-sector Series A companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median across this set is $300M, with a range of $150M–$500M.

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated valuation of $225M (median), with a range of $128M–$345M. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative end, reflecting typical Seed-stage dilution patterns, while peer comparables support the higher end, given that defense-sector companies at Tenna's stage often command substantial premiums. The primary uncertainty remains comparability: three peer data points are limited, and the timing and terms of peer raises are unspecified. Capital requirements and path to Series A execution also remain key variables affecting realized valuation.
